name: Seaway a division of Self-Help Federal Credit Union
description_short: Self-Help Federal Credit Union's Roseland Branch in Chicago, formerly part of Seaway Bank, offers checking, savings, personal loans, and small business lending to credit union members.
description_long:
Self-Help Federal Credit Union operates the Roseland Branch located at 11116 S Michigan Avenue in Chicago, Illinois. The branch has deep roots in the community, having originated as part of Seaway Bank before being integrated into Self-Help's multi-state credit union network. This heritage reflects the organization's commitment to serving historically underserved communities through accessible financial services.

The Roseland Branch provides core banking services including account opening, checking and savings accounts, personal loans, and small business loans. Members also have access to ATM services at the branch location. The credit union maintains routing number 322273696 for wire transfers and ACH transactions. The branch operates during extended hours, including Saturday morning service, to accommodate working members' schedules.

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Self-Help operates on a member-owned cooperative model, which typically translates to competitive rates and lower fees than commercial banks. The organization serves multiple states (California, Illinois, Washington, Wisconsin, and South Carolina) and maintains a blog and investment resources for member education. The company emphasizes account security, member support, and community engagement through its Video Vault historical content.

Prospective members should note that availability of specific products, rates, and services may vary by membership eligibility and state regulations. The website content provided focuses primarily on branch location and basic service availability. Detailed rate information, membership requirements, and comprehensive product comparisons would require additional research or direct contact with the credit union.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Extended branch hours including Saturday 9 AM–1 PM service for working members
Member-owned credit union model typically offers competitive rates and lower fees than traditional banks
Offers small business loans alongside personal lending, serving entrepreneurial members
Multi-state presence across five states provides network stability and geographic reach
Long community history originating from Seaway Bank demonstrates local commitment
ATM access at branch location for convenient cash withdrawals
Account security support and member help services available via phone (877-369-2828)
cons: Limited information on website about specific rates, fees, or APR terms for loans and accounts
Membership eligibility requirements not clearly detailed on the branch page
No online account opening option mentioned; requires in-person branch visit
Website navigation issues (404 errors) suggest outdated or incomplete online infrastructure
No mobile banking or digital service details provided on the Roseland Branch page
best_for: Chicago-area residents seeking community-focused banking with local roots
Small business owners looking for personal and business loan options in one institution
Credit union members in Illinois seeking extended branch hours including weekend access
diagnosis: The Roseland Branch is best for Chicago-area residents and small business owners seeking traditional credit union banking services with community history and extended branch hours. The main caveat is that detailed rate comparisons, membership eligibility criteria, and digital banking features are not clearly documented on the available website content, requiring direct contact with the credit union for complete information.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
